category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

surface.name: Set up and test drive Flutter
surface.selection_basis: Flutter's install documentation labels this page Quick start, recommends its VS Code route, and states a bounded achievement: install prerequisites and Flutter, create an app, run it on the web, and try stateful hot reload. The page is configured to Windows by default, which supplies the representative primary path.

alternatives_considered.name: Custom Flutter setup
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The custom setup page tells VS Code users to consider the quick start instead and is intended for other editors, manual setup, or particular target platforms.

alternatives_considered.name: Create a new Flutter app
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This reference assumes Flutter is already set up, while the selected quick start covers a new developer from installation through a running and hot-reloaded app.

alternatives_considered.name: Write your first app
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This longer codelab assumes setup is complete and claims about 90 minutes, while the quick start is the recommended first setup and test-drive surface.

entry_point.developer_intent: Install Flutter with VS Code on Windows and test the developer experience by creating, running, and hot-reloading the sample counter app.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected Flutter.
The representative developer is using Windows and does not yet have Git, VS Code, or Flutter configured.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The generated Flutter counter app runs in Chrome, retains its current counter state across a source edit, and uses the edited decrement behavior after stateful hot reload.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: After incrementing the counter, changing _counter++ to _counter--, and saving or clicking Hot Reload, the existing value remains and the next click decreases it.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The quick start's achievement list includes trying stateful hot reload, and its hot-reload section ends by telling the developer to notice that the value decreases instead of increasing.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the last observable behavior in the page's stated quick-start achievements. The following Flutter-sidebar exploration is product orientation after the app already runs and hot reload has succeeded.

prerequisites.requirement: A Windows computer supported by the current Flutter toolchain, with internet access and permission to install software and write the SDK and project files.
Git for Windows and Visual Studio Code, or another Code OSS-based editor compatible with the Flutter extension.
Google Chrome for the selected web execution route.

primary_path.action: Open the Flutter quick start and keep Windows selected as the development platform.
details: The guide also offers macOS, Linux, and ChromeOS variants.
primary_path.success_signal: Windows is shown as the configured platform.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choosing another operating system changes prerequisite installation actions but not the later VS Code test drive.

primary_path.action: Download and install the latest Git for Windows.
details: Skip only if Git is already installed.
primary_path.success_signal: Git is available for Flutter SDK cloning.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Git installation or PATH problems block the VS Code SDK download.

primary_path.action: Download and install Visual Studio Code.
details: A compatible Code OSS-based editor may substitute for VS Code.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Installation policy or permissions can block the editor setup.

primary_path.action: Open VS Code and install the Flutter extension from its Marketplace page.
details: Installing the Flutter extension also provides the Dart tooling used by the guide.
primary_path.output: Flutter commands available in VS Code
primary_path.success_signal: The command palette can surface Flutter commands.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Marketplace, network, or extension-installation failures block the guided SDK setup.

primary_path.action: Open View > Command Palette, type flutter, and select Flutter: New Project.
details: Because no SDK is configured yet, VS Code asks the developer to locate it.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the command is absent, verify the Flutter extension installation.

primary_path.action: Select Download SDK, choose an installation folder, and click Clone Flutter.
details: VS Code downloads the SDK into the selected folder.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The download can take a few minutes; if it appears hung, click Cancel and restart the installation.

primary_path.action: Click Add SDK to PATH after the SDK clone completes.
details: VS Code displays a success notification when the path update succeeds.
primary_path.success_signal: The notification says the Flutter SDK was added to PATH.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A failed PATH update prevents new terminals from finding flutter.

primary_path.action: Respond to the optional Google Analytics notice, then close and reopen all terminals and restart VS Code.
details: Consent is optional; the restart is required so all terminals receive the updated PATH.
primary_path.success_signal: A new terminal can resolve Flutter tooling.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An Android Studio warning can be ignored for the selected web route; run flutter doctor to verify installation if needed.

primary_path.action: Open the command palette again and select Flutter: New Project.
details: An operating-system or editor documents-access prompt may appear; agree to proceed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Denied filesystem access prevents project creation.

details: The guide says this bootstraps a simple counter app.
primary_path.success_signal: VS Code asks for the project parent directory.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choosing a different template changes the documented output.

primary_path.action: Select or create the parent directory and confirm Select a folder to create the project in.
details: Select the parent directory, not a pre-created project folder.
primary_path.success_signal: VS Code prompts for an app name.

primary_path.action: Enter trying_flutter or another lowercase_with_underscores project name and press Enter.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An invalid name must be replaced with one following the documented naming convention.

primary_path.action: Wait while VS Code runs Flutter project initialization and retrieves dependencies.
details: Progress appears in a notification and the Output panel.
primary_path.output: Generated counter app opened in VS Code
primary_path.success_signal: Initialization finishes and lib/main.dart is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Dependency download and project initialization can take time; inspect the Output panel for failures.

details: The generated comments explain the counter sample.
primary_path.success_signal: main.dart is open in the editor.

primary_path.action: Open the command palette, select Flutter: Select Device, and choose Chrome.
details: The quick start deliberately uses the web route even though Flutter targets several platforms.
primary_path.output: Chrome selected as the Flutter target
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If Chrome is unavailable, install it before selecting the web target.

primary_path.action: Select Run > Start Debugging or press F5.
details: Flutter builds and starts the app with flutter run, then opens a new Chrome instance.
primary_path.output: Running Flutter counter app in Chrome
primary_path.success_signal: Chrome opens and displays the newly created counter app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Build, browser, or toolchain errors must be resolved before the app can open.

primary_path.action: Click the increment button several times while the app is running.
details: This creates nonzero state that the hot-reload check must preserve.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the button does not update, the app is not running as expected.

primary_path.action: In lib/main.dart, change _counter++ to _counter--, then save all files or click Hot Reload.
details: Do not restart the app; the check relies on retaining its current state.
primary_path.input: Running app with nonzero counter state
primary_path.success_signal: The existing counter value stays the same immediately after hot reload.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Compilation errors prevent the hot reload from applying.

primary_path.action: Click the counter button again and observe the value.
primary_path.success_signal: The retained counter value now decreases instead of increasing.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If it still increases, confirm the edited line was saved and hot reload completed successfully.

branches.path: Install Git through the recommended Xcode command-line-tools route, install VS Code, then rejoin the shared VS Code SDK setup.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes prerequisite installation and OS prompts but preserves the same generated web app and hot-reload boundary.

branches.condition: The development computer runs Linux or ChromeOS.
branches.path: Install the listed curl, git, unzip, xz-utils, zip, and libglu1-mesa packages; on ChromeOS first enable and update Linux support; then install VS Code and rejoin the shared route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds package-manager work and, for ChromeOS, Linux-environment setup.

branches.condition: The developer prefers another environment or manual installation.
branches.path: Use the custom setup guide and its editor, SDK, and target-platform instructions instead of this recommended VS Code flow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces much of the primary installation path and may select a different runtime target.

branches.path: Click Cancel and restart the Flutter installation sequence.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a failed download attempt and repeat before PATH configuration.

branches.condition: VS Code warns that Android Studio is not installed.
branches.path: Ignore the warning for web, iOS, or macOS targets and optionally run flutter doctor to verify the rest of the installation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids an unnecessary Android Studio installation for the selected Chrome route.

friction_gates.description: Git, VS Code, the Flutter extension, and the Flutter SDK must all be installed before project creation.
Cloning the Flutter SDK takes a few minutes and may require cancellation and restart if it hangs.
The SDK must be added to PATH, and terminals plus VS Code must be restarted to receive the new environment.
The operating system or VS Code can request access to the selected documents folder before scaffolding.
Project initialization retrieves dependencies and can take time before main.dart is available.
Chrome must be installed and selectable for the quick start's web target.

sources.title: Set up and test drive Flutter
sections_used: Run your app on the web
evidence_supported: Complete project, browser, and hot-reload journey

sources.title: Set up Flutter for your particular needs
sections_used: Set up an IDE or editor
evidence_supported: VS Code users directed toward quick start

sources.title: Set up web development
evidence_supported: Chrome or Edge browser requirement for web debugging

excluded_after_success: Flutter sidebar and Widget Inspector orientation
Android, iOS, macOS, Windows, and Linux target setup
